comments.text: The best currently known lower and upper bounds for $c(n)$ are due to Connor and Marmorino from their paper: Connor, P., Marmorino, P. Decomposing cubes into smaller cubes. J. Geom. 109, 19 (2018). https://doi.org/10.1007/s00022-018-0424-4 Lower bound: For $n \geq 3$,\[ c(n) \;\geq\; 2^{\,n+1} - 1, \]so the lower bound is only exponential in $n$. Upper bound: If n+1 is composite, then,\[ c(n) \;\leq\; e^{2}\,n^{\,n}, \\[6pt] \]If n+1 is prime, then,\[ c(n) \;\leq\; 1.8\,n^{\,n+1}. \]
